danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring combat/dispatch; Bowser's operatives conducting theft operations

danger_signals.visual_form: Pokey sprite positioned at level exit
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental barrier requiring summoned ally (Yoshi) to progress; gates advancement toward Bowser's castle

danger_signals.visual_form: Antarctic castle in world map, fortress structure
danger_signals.meaning: Central threat location; Bowser's occupied territory; Mario is held captive within

danger_signals.visual_form: Empty pedestal or location in city; visual gap where landmark should display
danger_signals.meaning: Theft has occurred; world-state degradation; restoration objective flagged

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated NPC location within city areas
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for artifact authentication; quest objective marker; knowledge source for city identification

safety_signals.visual_form: Yoshi sprite accompanying or summoned to Luigi
safety_signals.meaning: Ally support; capable of eating Pokey obstacles; required for progression; symbol of partnership

safety_signals.visual_form: Non-Koopa inhabitants in city areas offering dialogue
safety_signals.meaning: Information sources; guides providing clues about artifacts and city identity; represents civilian cooperation
Safe mobility tool controlled by Luigi; enables city-to-city travel; represents operational advantage

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Koopa sprite; shell design; Antarctic castle aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Koopa sprites carrying stolen artifacts; Koopalings as boss encounters; Pokey variants blocking progression
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Antarctica location; Bowser's castle headquarters; PORTALS teleportation nodes; cities where Koopas operate with stolen goods

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Luigi protagonist sprite; Yoshi companion dinosaur; Mushroom Kingdom origin
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Real-world cities (Cairo, Paris, Tokyo, NYC, Mexico City, Buenos Aires); tourist information centers; photo album documentation of restored artifacts

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Local NPC sprites providing dialogue in each city
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: City streets and landmarks; information centers; artifact locations; knowledge about local treasures and city identity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Missing landmark on city map; absent artifact at expected location; visual void where treasure should display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Koopa patrol with stolen goods; NPC dialogue referencing theft; quest marker active for recovery

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Artifact restored to location; photograph taken of Luigi with restored treasure; visual completion of landmark
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Tourist information center attendant confirms authenticity; artifact placed in photo album; quest objective completed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progressive restoration of visible artifacts in city; landmark visual clarity increases as treasures return
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPC dialogue shifts from distressed (theft) to grateful (restoration); multiple Koopa encounters completed per city

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Koopalings positioned as floor guardians in castle; increasingly complex boss encounters; castle interior progression reveals
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss battles after 5-city recovery milestones; Bowser direct confrontation at castle apex; final cannon execution sequence (SNES variant)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mario absent from world; locked cell in castle; key item gated behind Bowser defeat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Yoshi summoning required to progress; Pokey obstacle as physical manifestation of captivity barrier; rescue finale triggers upon Bowser's defeat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Real-world landmarks missing from multiple cities simultaneously; world map showing distributed theft across continents
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Coordinated Koopa operations across Cairo, Paris, NYC, Tokyo, Mexico City, Buenos Aires; PORTALS system enabling systematic looting

environmental_storytelling.example: Big Ben missing from London area; Mona Lisa absent from Paris; Great Sphinx of Giza removed from Cairo—visual voids communicate theft and establish recovery objectives without explicit exposition
Authentic city locations (Cairo, Paris, Tokyo, NYC, Buenos Aires, Mexico City) serve dual purpose: geography education and immersion; player must identify cities through visual/dialogue cues
Local citizens provide clues about stolen artifacts and city identity; tourist information center attendants authenticate recovered treasures; dialogue-driven investigation reveals global conspiracy
Bowser's Antarctic fortress grows more defended as player advances; Koopalings guard successive floors; visual complexity increases toward Bowser's inner chamber, communicating mounting threat
Successful artifact restoration triggers photograph mechanic; visual album accumulation communicates progress; artifact images serve as tangible proof of recovered world-state
Pokey blocking castle exit (requiring Yoshi's consumption) represents enforced cooperation mechanic; physical obstacle reflects game's design emphasis on partnership and support systems

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Carries stolen artifact; patrols city areas; requires combat dispatch to obtain treasure; sprite animation reflects movement with cargo
Drops carried artifact; sprite explodes (per unused alternative mechanics in ROM); cleared from level area
Orchestrates global theft; maintains castle headquarters; uses PORTALS to coordinate Koopa operations; holds Mario imprisoned
Attempts direct attack on Luigi (MS-DOS/Macintosh variant); vulnerable after shell removal; defeated by environmental cannon launch (SNES variant) or wall kick (DOS/Mac variant)
Travels via Globulator map system; consumes Pokey obstacles; enables progression; visual indicator of summoned ally presence
Requires Globulator control to reposition across world map; sprite shows sticking tongue out animation (per unused graphics); reinforces separation mechanic
Engages NPC dialogue; identifies city location through clues; locates and defeats Koopas carrying artifacts; interacts with tourist information centers
Teleports via PORTALS; confronts Koopalings on successive floors; summons Yoshi at progression gates; moves toward Bowser confrontation
Provides dialogue clues about city identity and stolen artifacts; tourist center attendants ask authentication questions; offers world-state information
Blocks level exits; positioned as gate between player and progression; requires Yoshi summoning for removal; cannot be defeated by Luigi directly

notes: Mario is Missing! communicates world-state primarily through absence and restoration mechanics rather than traditional danger/safety visual design. The game's educational framework (real-world landmarks, geography focus) drives manifestation: stolen artifacts = visual voids, recovered artifacts = photograph documentation. Faction distinction is minimal (Bowser's Koopas vs. civilization) with emphasis on global cooperation narrative. Unlike traditional Mario games, danger signals are weak (Koopas are collectible obstacles, not lethal threats); safety signals emphasize partnership (Yoshi) and knowledge systems (NPC dialogue, tourist centers). Political situation manifests as coordinated global theft (Bowser's PORTALS system disrupting world landmarks), solved through artifact recovery and international cooperation. The game's design reinforces educational objectives: learning city locations, identifying landmarks, and understanding global interconnectedness through restoration gameplay.
